content.text: I’ll trace the token policy path in this repo and compare it to the logged principal/request, especially the `repository` deny reason.

The log already tells us policy evaluation reached the checked-in rule matcher and failed before any GitHub App installation token was minted. I’m opening the rule file and deny-reason logic now to identify the exact mismatch.

In the checked-out code, `chikachow/cyspbot-deploy` is already configured as an allowed principal/resource pair. A live denial with only `deny_reasons: ["repository"]` means the Worker version that handled the request almost certainly did not have that rule loaded, so I’m checking whether this rule is newer than the deployed script version.

The blame confirms the `cyspbot-deploy` allow rule was added in commit `6104c7b` today. Because the deployed Worker log says no rule existed for that principal repository, the failing request looks like it hit a production Worker version older than that commit or a deployment built from different policy contents.

There’s also a test that explicitly asserts this exact production rule should allow the request. I’m going to run just that policy test so the answer can separate “code says allow” from “deployed Worker denied.”

content.text: The focused test didn’t run because `pnpm` wanted to purge/reinstall `node_modules` and aborted without a TTY. I’m not going to force that in this workspace just to answer this; the static policy path is already enough to explain the failure.

I’ve got enough to answer: this is not an OIDC claim mismatch, scope parsing failure, or GitHub App permission problem. It’s the live Worker’s loaded Token Policy lacking any rule for `chikachow/cyspbot-deploy`.

It failed before GitHub token issuance because Token Policy denied the principal repository.

In [token-policy.ts](/Users/cysp/src/cyspbot/workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/policy/token-policy.ts:199), `deny_reasons: ["repository"]` is only emitted when there are zero configured rules whose `principalRepository` equals the verified OIDC principal’s `repository`.

Your logged principal was:

```text
repository: chikachow/cyspbot-deploy
workflow_ref: chikachow/cyspbot-deploy/.github/workflows/update-cyspbot.yml@refs/heads/main
ref: refs/heads/main
event_name: workflow_dispatch
```

The current checkout does have an allow rule for exactly that in [token-policy-rules.ts](/Users/cysp/src/cyspbot/workers/cyspbot-token-exchange/src/policy/token-policy-rules.ts:15), added by commit:

```text
6104c7b feat(token-exchange): allow cyspbot-deploy’s update workflow
```

So the most likely explanation is: the production Worker version that handled request `a1207dd84d0189cc` was not deployed from commit `6104c7b` or otherwise did not include that policy rule. If it had the current checked-in policy, this request should pass the repository, event, ref, workflow, resource, and permission checks.

I tried to run the focused policy test, but `pnpm` attempted to purge/reinstall `node_modules` and aborted without a TTY, so I did not force it. The static code path is still conclusive for the logged `repository` deny reason.
